title: COMPAT — Constraint-Market Compatibility: Heating Equipment (except Warm Air Furnaces) Manufacturing (NAICS 333414)

sections.title: Step 07 — Product Constraints (from upstream)
sections.content:
Constraints carried forward from 01_product_decomposition. No web search augmentation per instructions.

| # | Constraint | Type | Severity | Threshold | is_absolute |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| C1 | Media Limitation | physical | hard | Water/water-like only | true |
| C2 | Maximum Medium Temperature | physical | hard | 90 °C | true |
| C3 | Maximum Operating Pressure | physical | hard | 10 bar | true |
| C4 | Minimum Flow Rate Detection | operational | soft | 0.25–4.2 l/min (variant) | false |
| C5 | Air Bubble Tolerance | physical | soft | ~2% vol | false |
| C6 | Pipe Full Condition | operational | hard | Pipe must be full | true |
| C7 | Minimum Straight Pipe Run | operational | soft | 5–10× DN | false |
| C8 | Temperature-Dependent Accuracy | physical | soft | ~0.2% with NTC comp. | false |

---

sections.title: Step 08 — Constraint-Market Assessment: NAICS 333414
sections.content:
**Market context:** OEMs manufacturing hydronic heating equipment — gas condensing/non-condensing boilers, oil-fired boilers, electric hydronic boilers, baseboard heaters, radiant floor systems, solar water heating, and pool/spa heaters. Marquardt's sensor sits at L4.2.5.1 (Ultrasonic Flow Sensor) under L5.2.5 Flow Sensing & Measurement within L6.2 Hydraulic Distribution. [SRC: VN_heating_equipment_except_warm_air_furnac]

Operating environment: Closed-loop hydronic circuits, DN15–DN25 pipe, water or water-glycol media, 40–80 °C typical flow temperatures (non-condensing boilers up to 82–85 °C), 1–3 bar residential / up to 6 bar commercial, flow rates 5–30 l/min per zone. [SRC: 05_market_discovery Rank 1, VN L6.2]

### Verdict Matrix

| # | Constraint | Verdict | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| C1 | Media Limitation | **none** | Hydronic heating circuits circulate water or water-glycol mixtures exclusively. No oils, gases, or non-polar liquids present. Exact media match for sensor capability. [SRC: 01, VN L6.2] |
| C2 | Max Medium Temperature (90 °C) | **none** | Condensing boilers operate at 35–60 °C return / 55–80 °C flow. Non-condensing boilers reach 82–85 °C max flow temperature. Radiant floor systems run at 30–45 °C. All within the 90 °C rated limit with 5–55 °C headroom depending on output type. [SRC: VN L6.1, L6.3] |
| C3 | Max Operating Pressure (10 bar) | **none** | Residential hydronic systems operate at 1–3 bar with safety relief valves set at 3 bar. Commercial systems up to 6 bar. Well within 10 bar limit. [SRC: VN L5.2.6] |
| C4 | Min Flow Rate Detection | **none** | Boiler circulation flows are 5–30 l/min per zone at DN15–DN25. Minimum detectable (4.2 l/min DN20, 0.25 l/min DN14) is below typical operating range. Even low-load modulating boilers maintain circulation above detection threshold. [SRC: 01 specs, 05 Rank 1] |
| C5 | Air Bubble Tolerance (~2% vol) | **none** | Closed-loop hydronic heating systems are filled, purged, and pressurized at commissioning. Properly designed systems with expansion vessels (L5.2.6) and automatic air vents (L5.2.2) maintain <0.5% air content. Chronic air entrainment is a system defect addressed by water treatment (L5.2.7), not a routine operating condition. [SRC: VN L5.2.6, L5.2.7] |
| C6 | Pipe Full Condition | **none** | All hydronic heating circuits are closed-loop, fully filled, and pressurized as a prerequisite to operation. Partially filled pipes are a fault state detected during commissioning (L6.6). Fill valve and backflow preventer (L4.2.6.4) maintain system pressure. [SRC: VN L5.2.6] |
| C7 | Min Straight Pipe Run (5–10× DN) | **mitigable** | Inside boiler casings and compact mechanical rooms, space for 100–250 mm straight run (5–10× DN20) upstream of the sensor can be constrained. OEM integration within packaged boiler enclosures requires deliberate piping layout. Standard challenge for any inline sensor in this market — well understood by heating equipment designers. |
| C8 | Temp-Dependent Accuracy | **none** | Hydronic heating operates in a moderate, predictable temperature range (30–85 °C) with gradual ramp-up/down cycles. No extreme thermal transients. Built-in NTC compensation reduces error to ~0.2%, adequate for boiler modulation control and energy efficiency calculation. [SRC: 01 constraints, VN L6.4] |

### C7 Mitigation Detail

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Mitigation actions** | 1. Specify sensor mounting position in boiler hydraulic design (L6.2) to ensure adequate straight run — standard practice for OEM component integration. 2. Use integrated flow straightener vane upstream (<$5 BOM cost in OEM volume, adds ~2× DN equivalent). 3. Select DN14 variant for compact boiler platforms (shorter 120 mm body, reduced absolute straight-run requirement). |
| **Cost estimate** | low (<$10K) |
| **Time estimate** | 0 — addressable at OEM design phase with no product modification |

---

sections.title: Summary
sections.content:
| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| **Total assessments** | 8 |
| **Knockouts** | 0 |
| **Mitigable** | 1 (C7 — Minimum Straight Pipe Run) |
| **None (irrelevant)** | 7 |
| **Market status** | **SURVIVING** — no knockouts, proceeds to downstream scoring |

**Conclusion:** NAICS 333414 presents zero physics or regulatory barriers to Marquardt's ultrasonic flow sensor. The only relevant constraint is straight pipe run (C7), a standard OEM integration consideration mitigable at near-zero cost during boiler hydraulic design. This aligns with the market's Tier 1 near-adjacent classification (architecture distance = 2) — the closest market to the home market (333415). Media, temperature, pressure, and flow ranges are a near-perfect match: hydronic heating circuits operate squarely within the sensor's design envelope. The market can be entered with the current product, no redesign required. [SRC: 05_market_discovery Rank 1, VN_heating_equipment_except_warm_air_furnac]

---

rows.Rationale: Hydronic heating circuits circulate water or water-glycol mixtures exclusively. No oils, gases, or non-polar liquids present. Exact media match for sensor capability. [SRC: 01, VN L6.2]

Condensing boilers operate at 35–60 °C return / 55–80 °C flow. Non-condensing boilers reach 82–85 °C max flow temperature. Radiant floor systems run at 30–45 °C. All within the 90 °C rated limit with 5–55 °C headroom depending on output type. [SRC: VN L6.1, L6.3]
Residential hydronic systems operate at 1–3 bar with safety relief valves set at 3 bar. Commercial systems up to 6 bar. Well within 10 bar limit. [SRC: VN L5.2.6]

Boiler circulation flows are 5–30 l/min per zone at DN15–DN25. Minimum detectable (4.2 l/min DN20, 0.25 l/min DN14) is below typical operating range. Even low-load modulating boilers maintain circulation above detection threshold. [SRC: 01 specs, 05 Rank 1]

Closed-loop hydronic heating systems are filled, purged, and pressurized at commissioning. Properly designed systems with expansion vessels (L5.2.6) and automatic air vents (L5.2.2) maintain <0.5% air content. Chronic air entrainment is a system defect addressed by water treatment (L5.2.7), not a routine operating condition. [SRC: VN L5.2.6, L5.2.7]

All hydronic heating circuits are closed-loop, fully filled, and pressurized as a prerequisite to operation. Partially filled pipes are a fault state detected during commissioning (L6.6). Fill valve and backflow preventer (L4.2.6.4) maintain system pressure. [SRC: VN L5.2.6]

Inside boiler casings and compact mechanical rooms, space for 100–250 mm straight run (5–10× DN20) upstream of the sensor can be constrained. OEM integration within packaged boiler enclosures requires deliberate piping layout. Standard challenge for any inline sensor in this market — well understood by heating equipment designers.

Hydronic heating operates in a moderate, predictable temperature range (30–85 °C) with gradual ramp-up/down cycles. No extreme thermal transients. Built-in NTC compensation reduces error to ~0.2%, adequate for boiler modulation control and energy efficiency calculation. [SRC: 01 constraints, VN L6.4]

rows.Value: 1. Specify sensor mounting position in boiler hydraulic design (L6.2) to ensure adequate straight run — standard practice for OEM component integration. 2. Use integrated flow straightener vane upstream (<$5 BOM cost in OEM volume, adds ~2× DN equivalent). 3. Select DN14 variant for compact boiler platforms (shorter 120 mm body, reduced absolute straight-run requirement).
0 — addressable at OEM design phase with no product modification
**SURVIVING** — no knockouts, proceeds to downstream scoring
